001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml.*;
020import java.io.*;
021import java.lang.reflect.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
025
026/**
027 * TOML marshaller combining {@link TomlSerializer} and {@link TomlParser}.
028 *
029 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
030 * <p class='bjava'>
031 *    <jc>// Serialize a config map to TOML</jc>
032 *    Map&lt;String, Object&gt; <jv>config</jv> = Map.of(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"myapp"</js>, <js>"port"</js>, 8080);
033 *    String <jv>toml</jv> = Toml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>config</jv>);
034 *
035 *    <jc>// Parse TOML into JsonMap</jc>
036 *    JsonMap <jv>parsed</jv> = Toml.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>toml</jv>, JsonMap.<jk>class</jk>);
037 *
038 *    <jc>// Round-trip a bean</jc>
039 *    MyConfig <jv>config</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyConfig(<js>"myapp"</js>, 8080);
040 *    <jv>toml</jv> = Toml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>config</jv>);
041 *    MyConfig <jv>restored</jv> = Toml.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>toml</jv>, MyConfig.<jk>class</jk>);
042 * </p>
043 * <p class='bjava'>
044 *    <jc>// Using instance methods</jc>
045 *    Toml <jv>m</jv> = Toml.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
046 *    String <jv>toml</jv> = <jv>m</jv>.write(<jv>config</jv>);
047 *    JsonMap <jv>parsed</jv> = <jv>m</jv>.read(<jv>toml</jv>, JsonMap.<jk>class</jk>);
048 * </p>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output (Map of name/age):</h5>
051 * <p class='bcode'>
052 *    name = "Alice"
053 *    age = 30
054 * </p>
055 *
056 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (nested table + array):</h5>
057 * <p class='bcode'>
058 *    name = "Alice"
059 *    age = 30
060 *    tags = ["a", "b", "c"]
061 *
062 *    [address]
063 *    street = "123 Main St"
064 *    city = "Boston"
065 *    state = "MA"
066 * </p>
067 *
068 * <p class='bjava'>
069 * <jc>// Using static shortcuts.</jc>
070 *    MyPojo <jv>myPojo</jv> = Toml.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>string</jv>, MyPojo.<jk>class</jk>);
071 *    String <jv>string</jv> = Toml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>myPojo</jv>);
072 * </p>
073 *
074 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
075 *    <li class='link'><a href="https://toml.io/en/v1.0.0">TOML v1.0.0 Specification</a>
076 * </ul>
077 */
078@SuppressWarnings({
079   "resource" // Cursor shortcut methods return Closeables owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
080})
081public class Toml extends CharMarshaller {
082
083   /** Default marshaller instance. */
084   public static final Toml DEFAULT = new Toml();
085
086   /**
087    * Serializes a POJO to a <c>String</c> using the {@link #DEFAULT} marshaller.
088    *
089    * <p>
090    * A shortcut for calling <c><jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.write(<jv>object</jv>)</c>.
091    *
092    * @param object The object to serialize.
093    * @return The serialized object.
094    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
095    */
096   public static String of(Object object) throws SerializeException {
097      return DEFAULT.write(object);
098   }
099
100   /**
101    * Parses an input into the specified object type using the {@link #DEFAULT} marshaller.
102    *
103    * <p>
104    * A shortcut for calling <c><jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.read(<jv>input</jv>, <jv>type</jv>)</c>.
105    *
106    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
107    * @param input The input.
108    * @param type The object type to create.
109    * @return The parsed object.
110    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
111    */
112   public static <T> T to(String input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException {
113      return DEFAULT.read(input, type);
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Parses an input into the specified parameterized object type using the {@link #DEFAULT} marshaller.
118    *
119    * <p>
120    * A shortcut for calling <c><jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.read(<jv>input</jv>, <jv>type</jv>, <jv>args</jv>)</c>.
121    *
122    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
123    * @param input The input.
124    * @param type The object type to create.
125    * @param args The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
126    * @return The parsed object.
127    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
128    */
129   public static <T> T to(String input, Type type, Type... args) throws ParseException {
130      return DEFAULT.read(input, type, args);
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Opens a low-level {@link RecordReader} cursor over the specified input using the {@link #DEFAULT} marshaller.
135    *
136    * <p>
137    * A shortcut for calling <c><jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.readRecords(<jv>input</jv>)</c>.
138    *
139    * @param input The input.
140    * @return A new {@link RecordReader}.
141    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
142    */
143   public static RecordReader toRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
144      return DEFAULT.readRecords(input);
145   }
146
147   /**
148    * Opens a low-level {@link RecordWriter} cursor over the specified output using the {@link #DEFAULT} marshaller.
149    *
150    * <p>
151    * A shortcut for calling <c><jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.writeRecords(<jv>output</jv>)</c>.
152    *
153    * @param output The output.
154    * @return A new {@link RecordWriter}.
155    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
156    */
157   public static RecordWriter ofRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
158      return DEFAULT.writeRecords(output);
159   }
160
161   /** Creates using default serializer and parser. */
162   public Toml() {
163      this(TomlSerializer.DEFAULT, TomlParser.DEFAULT);
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Creates with custom serializer and parser.
168    *
169    * @param s The serializer.
170    * @param p The parser.
171    */
172   public Toml(TomlSerializer s, TomlParser p) {
173      super(s, p);
174   }
175}
